title: palhinhaea lehmannii
intro: Palhinhaea lehmannii, also known as the Chilean polypody, is a species of fern native to Chile and Argentina. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits associated with Palhinhaea lehmannii include relief from urinary tract infections, fever reduction, and the treatment of various skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried leaves in hot water, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
